title: Tender Springy Japanese Polish Sausage Ozoni
description: A buttery and springy Japanese dish featuring Polish sausage, served in a comforting pasta water emulsion, perfect for a sick day. This Buddhist temple friendly dish is made in the form of ozoni.
instructions: Bring the pasta water to a boil in a large pot.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and add the sliced Polish sausage.
Cook the sausage for 5 minutes, then remove it from the pot with a slotted spoon.
Add the shredded mozzarella cheese to the pot and stir until melted and well combined with the pasta water.
Add the butter, soy sauce, and mirin to the pot and stir until the butter is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Return the cooked Polish sausage to the pot and stir to coat with the sauce.
Transfer the ozoni to a serving bowl and garnish with chopped green onions and sesame seeds.
